claims.claim_text: Senate Bill 739 would provide unconditional and direct cash assistance of $1,000 a month to approximately 2,500 youth aging out of the Extended Foster Care Program in California.
The implementation cost of the pilot program under SB 739 is approximately $30 million per year, totaling $90 million over three years.
Direct cash assistance empowers individuals in ways that traditional social safety net programs do not.
The Stockton Economic Empowerment Demonstration (SEED) program supported 125 residents in obtaining stable employment and improving their physical and mental health.
Participants in the SEED program obtained full-time employment at more than twice the rate of non-participants.
Nearly 65% of transition-age youth in foster care reported having lost their jobs, with almost a quarter (23%) at risk of losing their housing.
Youth aging out of the foster care system often lack familial support and are at risk of falling into homelessness.
Transitional age youth without familial support frequently end up victimized or incarcerated for committing crimes to support themselves.
More than financial support, UBI empowers young people to decide how to spend the money to meet their specific needs.
